title: Satellite Orbit Determination
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Satellite Orbit Determination: Satellite orbit determination: estimate initial state vector from tracking data (range, range-rate, angles) using batch or sequential processing. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.ode.equations_of_motion…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~200; atmospheric_drag_uncertainty, SRP_modeling_error set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: rho(t) = |X_sat(t; x0) - X_gs|; X_sat integrated from EOM with SRP, drag, gravity harmonics
E.world_state_x: initial state vector x0 = (r0, v0) in ECI coordinates at epoch t0
E.observation_y: ground station range rho(t), range-rate rho_dot(t), angular observations for N_passes

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 6D_orbital_state_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 200); atmospheric_drag_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: position prediction error < 100 m at 24h for LEO with SRP+drag. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~200. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |atmospheric_drag_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_obs * N_state ** 2) for batch normal equations per iteration
